prompt:
Two related additions to the knowledge graph at `.the-valley/` (read `.the-valley/README.md` for the v0 convention, `.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md` for the adopted outcome-DAG sketch, and `design/user-scenarios.md` for the ladder, before writing).

## 1. New idea node: actors in the graph + demand pressure as anti-stall
Create `.the-valley/ideas/ida-<hash>-demand-pressure.md`, frontmatter status: **exploring** (not adopted — this is an experiment candidate), source: owner.

Capture the owner's idea FAITHFULLY as the core of the node (his framing): *agents — human and AI — can play parts in the outcome graph and be scheduled to unblock productions, so that there is a form of standing pressure toward producing the final node: an anti-stall mechanism.* Develop it in his register:
- **Heterogeneous actor pool.** Humans and AI agents are the same kind of participant: schedulable actors with capabilities. They differ in how dispatch reaches them — an AI agent is spawned (klaus-shaped); a human's attention is routed. Consequence worth stating plainly: the priority-layer question that the adopted outcome-DAG node split in two (work-scheduling vs attention-routing) partially REUNIFIES — attention-routing is work-scheduling for the actor class that cannot be spawned.
- **Pressure is standing, not event-shaped.** Demand propagates from live root outcomes down the `blocked_by` closure; the system continuously reconciles 'what should be moving but isn't' rather than dispatching once and forgetting. Level-triggered, not edge-triggered — the same reconcile-don't-just-react controller principle the rest of the architecture leans on.
- **Stall is the named failure mode**, which makes anti-stall designable. Sketch a small stall taxonomy (agent run died silently; blocked on an unresponsive human; dependency went stale; orphaned outcome with no live root) and candidate mechanisms (leases on in_progress with expiry; escalation to a different actor class; re-dispatch; and the inverse — outcomes with no path to a live root LOSE pressure and become garbage-collectible, an anti-clutter mechanism for free).
- **The Nix resonance.** This is 'nix build for work': you demand the final artifact and the graph schedules everything required, caching what's done. A Nix-native system whose work management mirrors its build semantics. One short paragraph.
- **Why it matters:** without pressure the graph is a passive tracker (an issue list with extra steps); pressure is what makes it a production *engine* — it's load-bearing for the 'recursive, transparent outcome-production engine' framing in the README.
- Body cross-links: the adopted outcome-DAG node (this extends it), architecture.md's outcome-DAG bet, and note that the experiment below is its first test. Keep it roughly the size of the existing idea nodes; mark clearly which parts are the owner's core idea vs. development of it.

## 2. Seed `.the-valley/outcomes/` with the REAL follow-up queue (the experiment)
This instantiates the idea immediately: the-valley's actual pending work becomes the first live outcome DAG, and the klaus coordinator session acts as the scheduler (manual v0 — computing the frontier, dispatching agents, pressuring human-blocked nodes, flagging stalls).

First, the convention README currently says 'edges later' — amend it: outcome nodes now carry `blocked_by` (list of node ids) in frontmatter, because the DAG experiment requires it. Keep everything else minimal.

Create outcome nodes (`oc-<hash>-<slug>.md`, status open unless noted; concise bodies — a few sentences each, linking relevant docs):
- **Root: 'S1 holds — repos on my infrastructure, never losable'** — body points at user-scenarios.md § S1's acceptance checklist as its definition of done. blocked_by: the four below.
- **'Requirements rewritten from the scenario ladder'** — the named follow-up; blocked_by: none (frontier).
- **'Roadmap re-derived against the ladder'** — blocked_by: the requirements node.
- **'Every push replicated: classic-laddie primary + Hetzner offsite + verified restore'** — the S1 infra work (lives in cosmo, tracked here); blocked_by: the Hetzner-decision node.
- **'Hetzner replication mechanism decided (git mirror vs ZFS send vs restic — or combination)'** — **this one blocks on the OWNER, a human actor**: note in the body that the actor is patflynn, the options table is in roadmap.md Phase 0, and this node is deliberately the experiment's first human-blocked frontier item. blocked_by: none (frontier).
Do NOT include already-completed work as open nodes; you may include one CLOSED node — 'Knowledge v0 seeded with real content' (status: done, closes-reference PR #6) — so the graph shows a completed production from day one.

## Constraints
- Follow the v0 convention exactly (frontmatter fields, hash-derived ids, directories). Bodies stay short — these are work nodes, not essays.
- Touch ONLY `.the-valley/` (idea node, outcome nodes, convention README amendment). No layered-doc changes.
- All relative links resolve.
- PR description: separate the two acts (capture vs experiment seed), state the experiment protocol in two sentences (coordinator-as-scheduler: frontier computed and dispatched each session; stalls flagged; human-blocked nodes surfaced), and note the owner can veto any node in review. No AI/assistant attribution, no Co-Authored-By.

Open a PR.
